field.label: Context Vary Bypass
field.comment: If certain context changes are global and cacheable, you can list their names in a comma-delimited string. LiteMage cache vary will bypass those context changes and will not save a separate cache copy. Example: current_layout, weltpixel_gtm.

field.label: Enable Custom Vary
field.comment: Allows a new visitor to get a properly cached page when there is a customized vary in place. For example, different currencies based on GeoIP.

field.label: Ignored Identity Block Names
field.comment: By default, an identity block will output cache tags. But there are some usages which defeat the purpose of cache tags, or which cause unnecessary server lookups. These usages include: an identity block that outputs many tags, or a block that appears on every page. To improve performance, you can ignore such blocks. Add the block names to the ignored list in a comma-delimited string.

field.label: Ignored Cached Tags
field.comment: Cache tags allow intelligent purging, as pages associated with a particular tag may be purged together. However, performance will degrade if too many tags are assigned to a page. To improve performance, some tags may be ignored. For example, tags that appear on every page are unnecessary, as a Purge All will have the same effect. Turn on LiteMage Debug to examine the tags shown on the header, and determine which ones may safely be ignored. Add these tags to the ignore list in a comma-delimited string.

field.label: Do Not Purge Category Tags when a Product is Updated
field.comment: By default, when a product is updated, a reindex will purge all parent category pages. In categories and parent categories with many active products, this can lead to constant purging. Select "Yes" to purge only the product tag. Then, only the product page and related pages containing the product will be purged. Category and parent category pages that do not contain this product will not be purged.

field.label: Purge Products after a Sale
field.comment: If the product's in-stock quantity is displayed, select "Always purge." If only the product's stock status is displayed, select "Only purge when out of stock." If no stock status is shown, then there is no need to purge the product. Note: when you purge a product tag, all related pages that display the product are also purged.

field.label: Purge Related Parent Products after a Sale
field.comment: If a simple product is purged after a sale, and it has a parent product, also purge the parent product.

field.label: Ignored Purge Tags
field.comment: Purge tags usually have corresponding cache tags. However some third party modules will send unmatched purge tags that are never used. To keep LiteSpeed server from having to track such unused tags, you can add them here, in a comma-delimited string. Use a * as a wildcard at the end of a tag to group tags with common starting characters. Note: Ignored Cache Tags are automatically included in this list. Only add tags that are not already in that list.

field.label: Disable CLI Purge
field.comment: When disabled, LiteMage Cache purge events initiated from the command line will be ignored. CLI purge can be temporarily disabled while the system is syncing with an outside ERP system. This avoids constant purge commands slowing down the sync process. After the sync, you can enable CLI purge again and then Purge All.

field.label: Enable Debug
field.comment: Prints debug information to var/log/litemage.log. You can temporarily enable debug response headers. Please disable for production usage.

field.label: Log Debug Messages Only for Listed IPs
field.comment: Output debug messages only for specified IPs. (Space or comma separated.) This will reduce logged data and may be easier for troubleshooting.

field.label: Log Backtrace for Purge Events
field.comment: Prints backtrace for LiteMage purge events. This will help troubleshooting unexpected purges.

field.label: Frontend Store ID
field.comment: When cache is flushed from the backend, a purge request is sent to the frontend store URL. In a multi-site and multi-store setup, specify the default frontend store ID here, to ensure that the correct URL is purged. Default value is 1.

field.label: LiteMage Server IP
field.comment: If LiteMage is behind a CDN or proxy server, use this setting to specify the location of the LiteMage Cache server. This way, a cache flush can access the LiteMage server directly, instead of going through the proxy.

field.label: Basic Authentication
field.comment: If your site uses Basic Authentication, the CLI flush command will send an HTTPS request. Without a username and password, the request will fail. Please input credentials in the "username:password" format.

field.label: Enable Cache Warmer
field.comment: Runs the LiteMage cache warmer queue. Keep disabled until queue sources and limits are configured.

field.label: URL Sources
field.comment: Sources used when building the scheduled warmup queue. Build Queue adds/updates selected sources and removes work from scheduled sources no longer selected. Queue Build cron applies this automatically, but it does not crawl URLs; Queue Process cron handles due rows separately. Run Build Queue manually to apply source changes immediately. Each source has a default priority below. Lower priority numbers run earlier. Purge-driven delta warmup is event-driven and controlled separately below.

field.label: Queue Process Cron Schedule
field.comment: Cron expression for litemage_warmup_process (Run Queue). Default is every five minutes. The schedule must use five cron fields and cannot run more often than every five minutes.

field.label: Queue Build Cron Schedule
field.comment: Cron expression for litemage_warmup_generate (Build Queue). Default is daily at 03:00 server time. Build Queue refreshes configured sources, removes disabled scheduled-source work, and cleans stale recently seen queue rows. It does not run the crawler; litemage_warmup_process handles due queue rows. The schedule must use five cron fields and cannot run more than once per day.

field.label: Batch Size
field.comment: Maximum number of due queue rows processed by one queue run.

field.label: Concurrency
field.comment: Maximum concurrent warmup requests per queue process.

field.label: Request Timeout
field.comment: Warmup request timeout in seconds.

field.label: Crawl Delay
field.comment: Delay between warmup requests in milliseconds.

field.label: Max Runtime
field.comment: Maximum time one queue run may spend processing queued URLs, in seconds. Set this lower than the warmer cron interval, for example 240 seconds for a 5-minute cron, so queue runs do not overlap.

field.label: Max Server Load Average
field.comment: Skip warmup when the 1-minute system load average is at or above this value. Use 0 to disable. Set this below the load where shopper traffic starts to slow down.

field.label: Max Attempts
field.comment: Maximum attempts before a queue row is marked failed.

field.label: Queue Limit Per Store
field.comment: Hard cap for generated source URLs per store before variant expansion. Each admitted URL is queued for all configured variants.

field.label: Regular Recrawl Interval
field.comment: Seconds between successful scheduled warmups. Use 0 or blank for auto from Magento public TTL: litemage_runner uses the TTL, while litemage_walker uses slightly less than the TTL. Set -1 to disable recurring scheduled recrawl. Purge-driven rows remain one-shot.

field.label: Scheduled Crawl Mode
field.comment: Default is litemage_runner for lighter scheduled crawls: LiteSpeed serves HITs and Magento is reached only on MISS. Use litemage_walker only when the scheduled crawl should force a Magento backend request even if the page is already cached; this can refresh and extend cached objects, but it is heavier.

field.label: Enable Purge-Driven Delta Warmup
field.comment: Event-driven warmup after LiteMage purge events using litemage_runner. Example: if product P123 is purged and Magento can resolve it to /some-product.html, that product URL is queued immediately as urgent warmup. Category and CMS page purges behave the same way when their direct frontend URLs can be resolved. Direct purge URLs use default priority 10, where lower numbers run earlier.

field.label: Store Warmup Policy
field.comment: Controls which store views participate in scheduled warmup, purge-driven delta warmup, and Purge All recrawl. Priority offset is added after source, URL, and variant priority; lower final numbers run earlier.

field.label: Sitemap Paths
field.comment: Optional sitemap paths or URLs, one per line. CSV format: path or URL, store IDs, source priority. Store IDs use pipe separators, for example var/litemage/warmup/sitemap.xml,1|2|3,100 or https://site2.com/sitemap.xml,4|5,120. Absolute sitemap URLs are warmed only for stores whose base host matches the URL host.

field.label: Text/CSV File Paths
field.comment: Optional local text or CSV files, one per line. Source format: var/litemage/warmup/file.csv,1|2|3,20. Paths must start with var/litemage/warmup/. File rows use URL, URL priority when stores are bound at the source level; relative URLs expand once per selected store, while absolute URLs must match a selected store base host.

field.label: Allowed Query Parameters
field.comment: Comma-separated public query parameter names that may be warmed, for example p for pagination. URLs with other query parameters are rejected instead of silently changed.

field.label: Magento URL Rewrite Entity Types
field.comment: Comma-separated entity types to include from Magento's url_rewrite table when generating the Magento URL Rewrites source. Defaults to product, category, and cms-page.

field.label: Text/CSV Source Priority
field.comment: Optional default priority for text/CSV file URLs. Accepted range is 0-9999. Lower numbers run earlier. Blank uses default 20.

field.label: Sitemap Source Priority
field.comment: Optional default priority for sitemap URLs. Accepted range is 0-9999. Lower numbers run earlier. Blank uses default 100.

field.label: Magento URL Rewrites Source Priority
field.comment: Optional default priority for Magento URL Rewrites generated from the url_rewrite table. Accepted range is 0-9999. Lower numbers run earlier. Blank uses default 200.

field.label: Purge Event Source Priority
field.comment: Optional default priority for purge-event warmup. Resolved entity URLs and reverse-index URLs use this urgent lane. Accepted range is 0-9999. Lower numbers run earlier inside the urgent tier. Blank uses default 10.

field.label: Non-default Currencies To Warm
field.comment: Select important enabled non-default currencies whose public HTML/prices should be warmed. This setting is global, but each selected currency is warmed only for stores where that currency is enabled and is not the store default. The store default currency is already covered by the Guest profile.

field.label: Representative Customer IDs
field.comment: Optional dedicated warmup customer IDs, separated by commas, spaces, or new lines. Use fake/admin-created accounts assigned to the customer groups that need logged-in or group-specific price warming.

field.label: Warmup Variant Limit
field.comment: Hard cap for all variants expanded per URL, including guest, selected currencies, and representative customers. This prevents accidental queue explosion.

field.label: Queue / Variant Map
field.comment: Toggle queues and variants here, then use Save Config. Run Build Queue afterward to apply changes to queued warmup URLs. Duplicated URLs from different queues are crawled at the highest priority and are not recrawled multiple times.

field.label: Enable Tag-to-URL Index
field.comment: Optional fallback used only when Purge-Driven Delta Warmup is enabled. Stores compact tag-to-URL mappings from cacheable crawler MISS responses, so later product/category purges can enqueue related URLs that cannot be resolved directly. Keep disabled unless direct purge warmup plus scheduled CSV/sitemap recrawls miss important pages.

field.label: Enable Recently Seen URLs Queue
field.comment: When the Tag-to-URL Index is enabled, Build Queue can add recently seen tagged URLs collected within the mapping TTL. This is independent from purge-driven urgent recrawls.

field.label: Recently Seen URLs Limit
field.comment: Maximum recently seen fallback URLs added during Build Queue. URLs already tied to direct product, category, or CMS page URL rewrites are skipped because purge entity warmup can queue those directly. This limit applies before variant expansion.

field.label: Recently Seen URLs Source Priority
field.comment: Default queue priority for recently seen URLs. Accepted range is 0-9999. Lower numbers run earlier. Blank uses default 300.

field.label: Tag Mapping Max Tags Per URL
field.comment: Maximum product/category entity tags stored for one URL.

field.label: Tag Mapping Max URLs Per Tag
field.comment: Maximum URLs retained for one tag before older mappings are dropped.

field.label: Tag Mapping TTL Days
field.comment: Number of days before tag-to-URL mappings expire.

field.label: Queue and Result Retention Days
field.comment: Number of days to keep warmup result history, terminal queue rows, and purge events.
